message:
fix(app): handle OpenAI-style YYYY-MM-DD date suffixes and 4-digit version
suffixes in model alias detection

The is_alias() function previously only recognized 8-digit date suffixes
(e.g., "20240101") as non-alias model identifiers. This missed two common
patterns used by providers:

1. OpenAI-style hyphenated dates like "gpt-4o-2024-11-20" where the date
   is split across three hyphen-separated segments (YYYY-MM-DD). These
   were incorrectly classified as aliases, causing duplicate model entries
   or incorrect model matching.

2. Short 4-digit version suffixes like "gpt-4-0125" which are versioned
   model identifiers, not aliases.

The fix adds early detection of the YYYY-MM-DD pattern by checking the
last three hyphen-separated parts, and also treats 4-digit all-numeric
suffixes as non-alias identifiers. The corresponding property-based test
is updated to match the new logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
